description:
You are assisting a network engineer who is troubleshooting sporadic latency that appears to be linked to clocks drifting between different monitoring systems.  
For a first-step sanity check, the engineer wants positive proof that the current shell can generate a timestamp in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC).

Your job  
1. Create a directory called /home/user/logs (if it does not already exist).  
2. Inside that directory create a file named utc_check.log.  
3. The file must contain one and only one line, produced by a single terminal command, holding the three-letter time-zone designator “UTC” as returned by the GNU date utility when run in UTC mode.

Required exact file content (including the trailing newline character and no additional spaces):  
UTC⏎

Verification rules the automated tester will follow  
• The file must be located at /home/user/logs/utc_check.log  
• The file must be readable by a normal user (no root privileges expected).  
• The file must contain exactly four bytes: the three ASCII capitals U, T, C followed by a single newline character (hex 0A).  
• No other data, whitespace, or extra lines are permitted.  
• The directory /home/user/logs must exist after the command completes.

Hint (do NOT reveal commands): this can be accomplished with a one-liner that tells date to print its time-zone abbreviation while forced to UTC and redirects the output to the required file path.
truth:
Expected artefacts after successful execution:  
1. Directory: /home/user/logs  
   • Permissions: 755 (drwxr-xr-x) or more permissive  
2. File: /home/user/logs/utc_check.log  
   • Permissions: 644 (-rw-r--r--) or more permissive  
   • Size: 4 bytes  
   • Exact byte sequence (hex): 55 54 43 0A  
     (ASCII “U”, “T”, “C”, newline)

The test harness will read the file with `cat /home/user/logs/utc_check.log` and expect the sole output line to be:
UTC

No other checks (e.g., system clock settings) are performed.
name: task_000000_427684b7
